Partnering 	for 	Profit
Doing 	the 	Deal
Module 	2

-- 1 of 22 --

You've 	Come 	a 	Long 	Way, 	Baby!
y You selected your niche with care ...
y You selected the best potential partners
in that niche ...
y You have proven your value ...
y You have broadened your field of influence ...
y It’s time to close the deal!

-- 2 of 22 --

Rules 	of 	the 	Road
z Create a win‐win
z Minimize or eliminate client’s risk
z Offer strategic value and/or freebies
z Clearly state client responsibilities
z Clearly state your responsibilities
z Include carrots and sticks
z Define basic metrics and reporting
z Avoid directorship status
z Avoid employee status
z Acknowledge difference between gross revenues,
net revenues and profit

-- 3 of 22 --

Rules 	of 	the 	Road
z Avoid/use the “instant violation” trap
z Exclusivity should always have a price
z Sign “non‐compete” agreements cheerfully
z Make “non‐disclosure” clauses as specific as possible
z Always have an attorney review your agreement
before you sign it
z Get hard copies with live signatures
z Provide copies to your CPA/tax preparer
z Listen carefully to client concerns
z Never pay for anything

-- 4 of 22 --

Rules 	of 	the 	Road
z Work through an LLC or corporation
z NEVER agree to have overhead, investment, owner
compensation , interest, taxes or amortization
deducted prior to the calculation of your
compensation
z If marketing and fulfillment costs are deducted,
adjust your commission percentage accordingly
z Have out‐of‐state clients wire your money to you
z If it’s not working, change it – FAST
z Protect the relationship at all costs

-- 5 of 22 --

Three 	Types 	of 	Promos 	...
1. Zero marketing and fulfillment costs
2. Marketing costs but zero fulfillment costs
3. Marketing costs and fulfillment costs

-- 6 of 22 --

Six 	Types 	of 	Work 	...
1. New Customer Acquisition Promos
2. Customer File Promos
3. House Copy
4. Marketing Consulting
5. Organizational/Procedural Consulting
6. Deal‐Making

-- 7 of 22 --

Seven 	Income 	Streams
1. Royalties
2. Commissions
3. Overrides
4. Retainers
5. Hourly Fees
6. Bonuses
7. Equity Interest

-- 8 of 22 --

When 	Working 	for 	Free
Makes 	Sense 	...
1. To create an unspoken sense of obligation
2. When you own the back end

-- 9 of 22 --

Compensation 	Options 	for
New 	Customer 	Acquisition 	Promos
1. Free
2. Hourly fee
3. Flat fee
4. Advance against royalty
5. Fee plus royalty

-- 10 of 22 --

Compensation 	Options 	for
New 	Customer 	Acquisition 	Promos
Royalty 	possibilities:
3 Cents per impression
3 Percentage of media budget
3 Percentage of gross or net revenues
3 Percentage of increase in gross or net revenues
3 Percentage of reduction in lead cost
3 Percentage of reduction in cost/sale
3 Percentage of increase in gross or net revenues
3 Override on lead revenues

-- 11 of 22 --

Compensation 	Options 	for 	Promos 	to
Existing 	Customers
1. Percentage of sales
2. Percentage of increase in monthly sales
3. Metric‐based bonuses

-- 12 of 22 --

Compensation 	Options
for 	House 	Copy
1. Free
2. Hourly billing
3. Flat fee
4. Advance against commission
5. Straight commission

-- 13 of 22 --

Compensation 	Options 	for
Marketing 	Consulting
1. Free
2. Hourly billing
3. Retainer against billable hours
4. Equity position

-- 14 of 22 --

Compensation 	Options 	for
Organizational/Procedural 	Consulting
1. Free
2. Hourly billing
3. Retainer against billable hours
4. Equity position

-- 15 of 22 --

Compensation 	Options
for 	Deal‐Making
1. Free
2. Fee‐per‐deal
3. Cents per lead
4. Commission on net sales

-- 16 of 22 --

Fine 	Points 	...
 The Magic Number
z Less than standard direct mail royalty
z Makes you a hero quickly and easily
z Magic; not sacred

-- 17 of 22 --

Fine 	Points 	...
 Revenue definitions*
z Gross Revenue: 	Total money produced by promo
z Adjusted Gross Revenue: 	Gross revenue minus
marketing costs
z Net Sales: 	Gross minus marketing, product, 	order‐
taking, delivery and refund costs
*NOT used uniformly – make sure definitions are included in your contract!

-- 18 of 22 --

Fine 	Points 	...
 Company profit definitions*
z Gross revenue: 	Total revenues generated without
deductions of any kind
z Gross profit: 	Gross revenue minus marketing and
fulfillment costs (does not include corporate overhead
or stockholder compensation)
z Net profit: 	Total revenue minus all costs except
interest, taxes and amortization (EBITA)
z Retained profit: 	Net minus interest, taxes and
amortization expenses
*NOT used uniformly – make sure definitions are included in your contract!

-- 19 of 22 --

Contractural 	Necessities
1. 	Cancellation 	Clause
z Either party may cancel at any time with 90 days written notice
z Royalties for completed work continue as long as work is used
2. 	Compliance 	Clause
z Client assumes all responsibility to ensure that marketing claims, offers and
products comply with prevailing legal & regulatory requirements
3. 	Hold‐Harmless 	Clause
z Client agrees not to hold you responsible for legal actions resulting for its
distribution of your work product
4. 	Indemnification 	Clause
z Client agrees to reimburse you for any legal expenses you incur as a result
of legal actions taken against him
5. 	Compensation 	Date 	& 	Penalty 	Clause
z Client agrees to pay interest on late payments

-- 20 of 22 --

Contractural 	Necessities 	Continued
7. 	Term
z One 	to 	two 	years 	for basic 	agreements
z Consider 	inflation 	adjustment 	for longer
periods
8. 	Signed 	by 	Owner

-- 21 of 22 --

Questions?

-- 22 of 22 --
